response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and 20 years of diverse technical experience, he lacks the three non-negotiable requirements for this CTO role: production-grade multi-agent AI systems (only 1-2 months at re:cinq on MCP infrastructure), proven ability to scale engineering teams from 10→30+ people (no evidence of team leadership scope), and fintech infrastructure expertise (only recently joined re:cinq in April 2025, prior experience in direct mail, GIS, and general SaaS). He is transitioning into the AI/fintech space but is 2-3 years away from CTO-level readiness in this domain.
strengths: Deep software craftsmanship foundation: 20 years across web, mobile, embedded, and backend systems with demonstrated TDD, CI/CD, and clean architecture practices
Currently gaining agentic AI exposure at re:cinq and contributing to MCP frameworks, showing commitment to learning production AI systems
Proven ability to work across the full technical stack (TypeScript/Node.js, C++, D, infrastructure, databases) and solve complex distributed systems problems at scale
concerns: No production multi-agent AI experience: only 1-2 months at re:cinq on MCP code generators, well below the 'demonstrated production experience' requirement for systems operating at fintech scale
No evidence of scaling engineering teams from 10→30+ people or building technical leadership layers, which is central to the CTO's engineering-leadership mandate

Minimal fintech domain knowledge: just onboarded to re:cinq in April 2025; prior experience in direct mail (Optilyz), GIS platforms, and general SaaS with no background in credit systems, payment flows, or regulated financial environments

key_factors.factor: Proven team scaling and engineering leadership
Fintech infrastructure and regulatory domain expertise
